channel.title: The Soul of a New Machine — Audiobook
channel.description: Full audiobook of "The Soul of a New Machine" by Tracy Kidder, narrated by AI voices. A Pulitzer Prize-winning account of a computer engineering team racing to build a new machine at Data General in the late 1970s.
channel.itunes:author: Tracy Kidder

image.title: The Soul of a New Machine — Audiobook

channel.copyright: Original content © Tracy Kidder. This recording is a personal AI-narrated project.

item.title: Chapter 8 — The Wonderful Micromachines
item.itunes:title: Chapter 8 — The Wonderful Micromachines
item.description: Chapter 8 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Microteam's culture comes into focus — the Micropit, the microbus, the outdoor microlounge. We meet the young engineers who gave everything their "micro" prefix, and see how the team's peculiar rituals and camaraderie sustained them through the grinding pressure of debugging Eagle.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 8 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The Microteam's culture comes into focus — the Micropit, the microbus, the outdoor microlounge. We meet the young engineers who gave everything their "micro" prefix, and see how the team's peculiar rituals and camaraderie sustained them through the grinding pressure of debugging Eagle.

item.title: Chapter 7 — La Machine
item.itunes:title: Chapter 7 — La Machine
item.description: Chapter 7 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The debugging phase begins and the pressure mounts. West's office becomes a cage as the April deadline looms. The Hardy Boys work double shifts, the schedule slips "a week a week," and West — the master debugger — must stay away from the lab to prove he trusts his team. Meanwhile, news arrives that North Carolina is way behind, making Eagle's success a corporate necessity.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 7 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The debugging phase begins and the pressure mounts. West's office becomes a cage as the April deadline looms. The Hardy Boys work double shifts, the schedule slips "a week a week," and West — the master debugger — must stay away from the lab to prove he trusts his team. Meanwhile, news arrives that North Carolina is way behind, making Eagle's success a corporate necessity.

item.title: Chapter 6 — Flying Upside Down
item.itunes:title: Chapter 6 — Flying Upside Down
item.description: Chapter 6 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's rules of the game: compete for resources, promise the impossible schedule, sign up, and maybe get a reward. The brass tells the Microkids that Eagle is late and everything depends on them now. The pressure turns up, and the new hires discover what they've really signed up for.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 6 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." West's rules of the game: compete for resources, promise the impossible schedule, sign up, and maybe get a reward. The brass tells the Microkids that Eagle is late and everything depends on them now. The pressure turns up, and the new hires discover what they've really signed up for.

item.title: Chapter 5 — Midnight Programmer
item.itunes:title: Chapter 5 — Midnight Programmer
item.description: Chapter 5 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Carl Alsing introduces the author to "midnight programming" — the text adventure game that has swept through the basement of Westborough. Through the lens of Colossal Cave, we see the peculiar mindset of computer engineers: their love of puzzles, their patience with complexity, and the strange joy they find in getting lost in twisty little passages.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 5 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Carl Alsing introduces the author to "midnight programming" — the text adventure game that has swept through the basement of Westborough. Through the lens of Colossal Cave, we see the peculiar mindset of computer engineers: their love of puzzles, their patience with complexity, and the strange joy they find in getting lost in twisty little passages.

item.title: Chapter 4 — Wallach's Golden Moment
item.itunes:title: Chapter 4 — Wallach's Golden Moment
item.description: Chapter 4 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Steve Wallach, a brilliant young engineer, has his moment in the spotlight as the Eagle project's architecture takes shape. The team grapples with the VAX's shadow, and the basement culture deepens with late-night debugging sessions and the peculiar rituals of computer engineering.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 4 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Steve Wallach, a brilliant young engineer, has his moment in the spotlight as the Eagle project's architecture takes shape. The team grapples with the VAX's shadow, and the basement culture deepens with late-night debugging sessions and the peculiar rituals of computer engineering.

item.title: Chapter 3 — Building a Team
item.itunes:title: Chapter 3 — Building a Team
item.description: Chapter 3 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West assembles his team of engineers in the basement of Westborough. We meet Carl Alsing, learn about West's pig roasts and his past at the Smithsonian, and see the culture of the Eclipse Group take shape — cubicles, late nights, and the peculiar language of computer engineers.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 3 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West assembles his team of engineers in the basement of Westborough. We meet Carl Alsing, learn about West's pig roasts and his past at the Smithsonian, and see the culture of the Eclipse Group take shape — cubicles, late nights, and the peculiar language of computer engineers.

item.title: Prologue — A Good Man in a Storm
item.itunes:title: Prologue — A Good Man in a Storm
item.description: The prologue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West, a mysterious computer engineer, reveals his character during a stormy sailing trip off the coast of Maine — a portrait of the man who will lead a team of engineers to build a new minicomputer at Data General.
item.itunes:summary: The prologue of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West, a mysterious computer engineer, reveals his character during a stormy sailing trip off the coast of Maine — a portrait of the man who will lead a team of engineers to build a new minicomputer at Data General.

item.title: Chapter 2 — The Wars
item.itunes:title: Chapter 2 — The Wars
item.description: Chapter 2 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West sneaks into a building to physically disassemble DEC's VAX computer, the Eagle project gets its name, and the internal "wars" at Data General over who would build the 32-bit machine are laid bare. A story of corporate espionage, engineering rivalry, and the race to build a supermini.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 2 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." Tom West sneaks into a building to physically disassemble DEC's VAX computer, the Eagle project gets its name, and the internal "wars" at Data General over who would build the 32-bit machine are laid bare. A story of corporate espionage, engineering rivalry, and the race to build a supermini.

item.title: Chapter 1 — How to Make a Lot of Money
item.itunes:title: Chapter 1 — How to Make a Lot of Money
item.description: Chapter 1 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The story begins at Data General, where Tom West and his team of engineers are racing to build a new 32-bit minicomputer — the Eagle — before the competition beats them to it. A deep dive into the culture, pressure, and personalities of the computer industry in the late 1970s.
item.itunes:summary: Chapter 1 of Tracy Kidder's Pulitzer Prize-winning "The Soul of a New Machine." The story begins at Data General, where Tom West and his team of engineers are racing to build a new 32-bit minicomputer — the Eagle — before the competition beats them to it. A deep dive into the culture, pressure, and personalities of the computer industry in the late 1970s.
